novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the advertised constructive PC-stable affine certificate is largely imported as Assumption 5 rather than produced as the §8 object, so the current flagship kernel is conditional on the promised object.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Theorem 2 / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked target and branch inequalities select max-absolute or largest-positive effects, but §4 and the consumer claim IDA lower-bound/minimum-absolute reporting; min-absolute selection is not the same affine event.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The headline promises a PC-stable discovery certificate, but Assumption 5 already assumes the learned CPDAG/MPDAG event is approximated by A_{0,n}xi<=b_{0,n} up to rho_n.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Given Assumptions 5 and 6, the proof route mostly stacks assumed affine discovery rows with Theorem 1 rows and applies Lee et al.; the non-assumption mathematical content is below flagship unless the PC-stable certificate is moved into the conjectured conclusion.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Move the PC-stable Fisher-z affine-certificate claim out of Assumption 5 and into Conjecture 1 as the object to be constructed, with input triples, row-generation rule, and a stated rho_n bound from §6 primitives.

Decide whether the report is max-absolute/largest-positive or Maathuis-style minimum-absolute lower bound; if the latter, add a sign-chamber union/polyhedral-conditioning result for min |theta_m| and update Theorem 2 accordingly.

Add one corollary explicitly translating the pivot to the actual IDA summary reported in MaathuisKalischBuhlmann2009IDA, including whether it covers the selected branch effect or the lower-bound summary itself.
Separate the generic Lee-pivot application from the novel causal-discovery certificate in the results-at-a-glance list so the flagship claim is not carried by Theorem 2 alone.

recommended_next_step: Revise the flagship conjecture so the PC-stable certificate is constructed rather than assumed, and align the selected report with either max-absolute or minimum-absolute IDA summaries.
